short_summary: Artificial marks feigned emotion and deceptive performance rather than neutral manufacture.

source_name: Thoughts on the Education of Daughters via Project Gutenberg
short_summary: Artificial is opposed to genuine feeling and signals affectation.

short_summary: Artificial can name a substitute object that resembles a natural counterpart without necessarily deceiving anyone.

source_name: The Port Gibson Reveille via Chronicling America
short_summary: Artificial enters food trust and additive scrutiny without becoming a straight synonym for fake.
Absence of artificial becomes a positive consumer claim and links artificial to additive suspicion.
Regulation stabilizes artificial as a label visible industrial category rather than a fraud word.
Modern labeling still treats artificial as a live contrast term in consumer food language.
An artificial thing can be realistic by resembling what is natural or real without becoming real itself.
Modern usage treats artificial flowers as capable of lifelike realism which suggests simulation and imitation rather than direct fraud.
notes: Media snippet not a balanced corpus.

short_summary: Simulated often points to modeled scenarios or training environments rather than generic human made materiality.
notes: Shows simulated is narrower than artificial.

short_summary: Imitation foregrounds copying a genuine article and sits closer to substitute logic than neutral artificial.
Natural is opposed to artificial in both origin and food processing discourse.
Real opposes counterfeit artificial and imitation directly and therefore belongs in the strongest contrast field.
